---
title: "Working from the Command Line"
toc: false
toc-location: body
---

Many genomics workflows are run from the **command line**, a text-based interface for interacting directly with your computer’s operating system.

Working from the command line allows you to:

- run scientific software and analysis pipelines  
- organize and manipulate large datasets  
- automate repetitive tasks  
- interact with remote computing resources such as **high-performance computing (HPC) clusters**

Although graphical interfaces are useful for many tasks, command-line tools provide **greater flexibility and control**, and they are the standard way to run most bioinformatics software.

The exact commands available depend on your operating system. Most HPC systems use **Linux**, while personal computers commonly run **Windows** or **macOS**. Because macOS and Linux are both **UNIX-based**, they share the same command-line tools and conventions.

:::{.highlight-section}
Select the tutorial that matches your operating system.
:::

:::: {.resource-row}

:::{.resource-card data-href="pages/resources/cmd_line/bash.html"}
![](/images/icons/bash.png){.resource-icon}

Linux / macOS (Bash / zsh)
:::

:::{.resource-card data-href="pages/resources/cmd_line/windows.html"}
![](/images/icons/windows.png){.resource-icon}

Windows (PowerShell)
:::

::::

<script>
document.querySelectorAll('.resource-card[data-href]').forEach(card => {
  card.addEventListener('click', () => {
    window.location.href = card.getAttribute('data-href');
  });
});
</script>
